nameMeanings.name: Nicholls
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Dutch: variant of   Nichols  .

variants.name: Nichols
meanings: English: variant of Nichol , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In North America, this surname has absorbed various cognates from other languages, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ); see also below.
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Italian Francesco , originally a vocabulary word meaning ‘French’ or ‘Frenchman’ (Late Latin Franciscus; compare Frank ). This was a nickname given to St Francis of Assisi ( 1181–1226 ) because of his wealthy father's business connections with France. His baptismal name was Giovanni . He had a pleasant, ordinary life as a child and young man, but after two serious illnesses, a period of military service, and a year as a prisoner of war in Perugia, he turned from the world and devoted himself to caring for the poor and sick. He was joined by groups of disciples, calling themselves ‘minor friars’ (friari minores). The main features of the Franciscan rule are humility, poverty, and love for all living creatures. The given name occurs occasionally in England as early as 1300 , and more frequently from the early 16th century, when there was a surge of admiration for, and imitation of, Italian Renaissance culture.

en.lifeSummary: When William Francis Nicholls was born in 1573, in Sedgeberrow, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Nicholas, was 24 and his mother, Ann Carter, was 19. He married Anne Carter in 1596. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1620, in his hometown, at the age of 47.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Francis Nicholls родился в 1573 г. в местности седжебирроу, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Nicholas было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Ann Carter, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Carter в 1596 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1620 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 47 лет.
